verdict_reasoning: KLK2 is predominantly a secreted serine protease, but recent systematic studies (2025) confirm that it achieves robust cell surface expression in prostate cancer cells, accessible to bispecific T-cell engagers, CAR-T, and radioligand therapy. The protein has dual localization: secreted into prostatic fluid (established function) and surface-localized in prostate cancer (documented by IHC, immunofluorescence, and FACS). The surface pool appears contextual to the malignant state or cell type (prostate epithelium/cancer cells) rather than a baseline classical surface receptor property, but the accessibility is well-documented and therapeutically validated.
